summary: Backend testing PASSED (14/14 tests). Frontend testing BLOCKED by critical 'Cannot use import.meta outside a module' JavaScript error that prevents React app from loading. The frontend shows only a loading spinner indefinitely.

critical_blocking_issues.issue: Frontend app stuck on loading spinner - never renders login page or main app
critical_blocking_issues.error: PAGE ERROR: Cannot use 'import.meta' outside a module
critical_blocking_issues.impact: CRITICAL - All frontend features are untestable

features_tested: Auth: Admin login, Demo login, Invalid credentials
Reports API: Default params, start/end dates, single day, week, month, quarter, year periods

frontend_features_NOT_testable: Reports page period filters (Today, Week, Month, Quarter, Year)
Offline Settings section in Admin Settings

code_review_findings.reports.tsx: Code structure looks correct - handlePeriodChange triggers fetchReports via useEffect, fetchReports calculates date ranges correctly
code_review_findings.reportsApi: API client correctly passes start_date and end_date parameters
code_review_findings.OfflineDB.ts: MODIFIED - Changed from eager initialization to lazy initialization with getDB() function to avoid SSR issues
code_review_findings.OfflineStatusIndicator.tsx: Code structure is correct with proper data-testid attributes
code_review_findings.SyncService.ts: Code correctly uses browser environment checks

action_items: CRITICAL: Fix 'Cannot use import.meta outside a module' error that blocks frontend from loading
The error may be from a node_modules package (likely expo-related) that uses import.meta
Consider checking metro.config.js transformer settings or babel presets
The import.meta error was mentioned as 'known issue' by main agent but it IS blocking app load now
After fixing, retest: Reports page filters, date picker, tabs, Offline indicator, Offline settings
critical_code_review_comments: OfflineDB.ts was modified to use lazy initialization (getDB() function) to prevent SSR issues - this fix should be retained
There are two offline sync services: offlineSyncService.ts (Zustand-based) and SyncService.ts (Dexie-based) - potential conflict
Multiple OfflineStatusIndicator components exist - may cause confusion
context_for_next_testing_agent: Frontend is completely blocked by 'import.meta' JavaScript error. Backend APIs work correctly. The OfflineDB.ts was modified to use lazy initialization but did not fix the import.meta issue. The error appears to originate from an expo/metro bundling issue, not from the app source code directly.

rca_of_the_issue.symptom: Frontend shows loading spinner indefinitely, React app never mounts
rca_of_the_issue.root_cause_hypothesis: JavaScript error 'Cannot use import.meta outside a module' is thrown at runtime, likely from an expo/metro package that was compiled incorrectly or uses ESM syntax in a CJS context
investigation_done: Searched all app source files - no direct import.meta usage found
Error originates from acorn parser (JS runtime parser) detecting import.meta usage
Modified OfflineDB.ts to lazy load Dexie - did not fix the issue
recommended_fix: Use web_search to find 'expo metro import.meta error 2025/2026' for latest solutions
Check if dexie package needs special babel/metro configuration for expo web
Consider adding babel plugin to transform import.meta to environment-specific code
May need to update expo to latest version or add specific polyfill
